name: Steve Taylor
description:
'''Roland Stephen Taylor''' (born December 9, 1957) is an American singer, songwriter, record producer, music executive, filmmaker, assistant professor, and actor. A figure in what has come to be known as [[Christian alternative rock]], Taylor enjoyed a successful solo career during the 1980s, and also served in the short-lived group [[Chagall Guevara]]. 

In contrast to many Christian musical artists, his songs have often taken aim at Christian hypocrisy with the use of satirical, sardonic lyrics. In 1997, he founded the record label [[Squint Entertainment]], which fueled the careers of artists such as [[Sixpence None the Richer]], [[Chevelle (band)|Chevelle]], and [[Burlap to Cashmere]]. Despite this success, Taylor was ousted from the label by its parent, [[Word Entertainment]], in 2001. 

Taylor has produced and written for numerous musical acts, one of the most consistent being [[Newsboys]]. As a film-maker, Taylor co-wrote, directed, and produced the feature films ''[[Down Under the Big Top]]'', ''[[The Second Chance]]'', and ''[[Blue Like Jazz (film)|Blue Like Jazz]]''. After a decade long hiatus, Taylor returned to performing music in 2010 as the front-man for [[Steve Taylor & The Perfect Foil]], a [[Supergroup (music)|supergroup]] he founded with [[Peter Furler]], [[Jimmy Abegg]], and [[John Mark Painter]]. Along with a university residency and continued filmmaking, Taylor resumed work on unfinished [[Chagall Guevara]] material into the 2020s.
